CONTENT.Club career: Črnigoj made his Slovenian PrvaLiga debut for Koper on 1 August 2012 against Aluminij, replacing Denis Popović after 87 minutes of the match. In August 2015, he transferred to Swiss Super League club Lugano.In August 2020, Črnigoj signed a three-year contract with Italian Serie B club Venezia. In November 2021, he extended his contract until 2025. On 26 January 2023, he signed for Serie A club Salernitana on loan until the end of the season with the option to make the move permanent.

CONTENT.Career statistics: As of 23 August 2023Scores and results list Slovenia's goal tally first, score column indicates score after each Črnigoj goal.
CONTENT.International career: On 23 March 2018, Črnigoj debuted for the Slovenia national team in a 3–0 away friendly defeat against Austria, coming on as a 77th-minute substitute for Josip Iličić.

SUMMARY: Domen Črnigoj (born 18 November 1995) is a Slovenian professional footballer who plays as a midfielder for Italian Serie B club Reggiana, on loan from Venezia.
